items.rationale: Viewed as crypto's first true product-market fit and 'killer app' — cheapest way to move dollars, path to bring billions onchain, and a market-based answer to USD-denominated CBDC/national-security concerns.
items.age_note: Conviction strengthened over time — framed as run-risk research in 2023, by late 2024 declared the clearest PMF in crypto.

items.rationale: Belief that AI needs blockchains for ownership, content provenance/authenticity against deepfakes, decentralized compute markets, and agent-to-agent payments. Treated as the defining new platform wave.
items.age_note: Emerged 2023, became a dominant theme in 2024 and the headline 2025 prediction.

items.rationale: Seen as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a way to democratize ZK for developers; team repeatedly calls ZK under-hyped and is shipping its own zkVM work.
User-owned-and-operated physical networks that centralized hardware can't replicate; pitched as the future of consumer crypto. Backed by the Daylight Energy investment.

The core ideological thesis — reduces trust, mitigates the risks securities laws target, enables competition and credible neutrality. Pushed to be enshrined in law as the limiting principle for token regulation.

Ethereum's rollup-centric scaling endgame; fee reductions now unlocking apps. Backed via EigenLayer (restaking/'verifiable cloud') and Espresso (shared sequencing). Fragmentation/interop is the open challenge.
Onchain IP layer to compensate creators as AI explodes content volume; NFTs as digital property rights and identity. Backed by $80M Story Series B.
Held that mainstream adoption is impossible without privacy that is also compliance-compatible; excited by Aleo, enclaves, and coSNARKs for private shared state.
Growing usage, DEX share rising vs CEXs, and maturing token value-accrual models (fee switches, buy-and-burn) seen as the path to sustainable network tokens.

Most intense recurring concern. Seen as deterring serious founders, arming politicized regulators, and reframing crypto as a casino — a structural drag on legitimacy and adoption with no productive value.
items.age_note: Persistent and escalating from 2023 through Jan 2025; not reversed.

items.rationale: Years of lawfare, the unprecedented application of Howey to secondary sales, the expanded 'dealer'/'broker' rules, and DeFi-targeting tax rules. Framed as anti-tech, offshoring innovation, a national-security risk.
items.age_note: Acute alarm through 2024 under Biden/Gensler SEC; cooled to cautious optimism after Nov 2024 election ('future of crypto in the U.S. is bright', 2024-11-10; 'best time to build', 2025-01-08). Residual fights remain (IRS broker rule, DAO liability).

items.rationale: Short/chunky lockups and non-dilutable fixed token allocations enable insiders to dump on retail and misalign incentives; advocated 4yr/1yr cliffs and decentralization-milestone unlocks.
Easy/early secondary liquidity (like SPACs and ICOs) produces vaporware and retail harm; disclosure alone won't fix it — long holding periods needed to incentivize long-term building.
A California ruling exposing DAO members to general-partnership liability for mere forum participation; treated as an urgent threat requiring legal wrappers (DUNA).
Portfolio companies — many pre-revenue and pre-token — losing bank accounts without justification; framed as a coordinated, under-acknowledged abuse.
PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block building via order-flow feedback loops; multi-block and cross-chain MEV could make it worse. Largely a research-grounded concern.
ZK circuits securing billions are riddled with bugs and protected mainly by obscurity; identified as the biggest threat to SNARKs reaching their potential.
Wholesale CBDC development doubling post-sanctions seen as a dollar-reserve/national-security threat; winner-take-all stablecoin regulation concentrates power and undermines decentralization.
Flight-to-safety dynamics, liquidity withdrawal during stress, and algorithmic death-spirals; regulators converging against algo stablecoins toward fiat-backed.
items.age_note: Detailed in Sept 2023 research; not prominently restated as stablecoin optimism rose through 2024, so the macro-bullish view now dominates.

items.rationale: Treated as definitively not a security (SEC implicitly conceded via ETH ETF/futures approvals), the bedrock for the rollup roadmap, and expected to be deflationary long-term.
Digital gold / store of value and 'the first Internet native religion'; respected as foundational — but the team is lukewarm on a US strategic Bitcoin reserve as a priority.
Transparent reserves, a flight-to-safety asset in stress events, and 'likely okay' under securities law (Circle isn't issuing yield to retail).
Cited as the top chain by monthly active addresses and a leader (with Base) in low-fee DEX volume — though flagged for sandwiching/MEV. Endorsement is implied by metrics, not explicit.
Ownership of nothing, no productive value, gambling — and a reputational/regulatory drag on the whole industry.
Less transparent reserves and elevated run-risk in stress periods; ironically used as a regulatory-arbitrage safe haven, which itself signals concern about asset quality.
items.age_note: Concern detailed in Sept 2023 research; not restated recently, but never reversed.

items.rationale: UST-style designs prone to death spirals; yield-bearing stablecoins risk securities-law treatment (the Terra/Anchor pattern).
items.age_note: Terra collapse repeatedly cited as the cautionary case; regulators converging against algo designs.

risk_regime.rationale: As of Jan 2025 the house is structurally bullish: State of Crypto metrics (mobile wallets, stablecoin volume, fees, DEX share) at all-time highs; infrastructure (cheap L2s, ZK, wallets) finally 'ready for apps'; stablecoins and crypto×AI at/near PMF; and the post-election regulatory cloud lifting ('best time to build'). Tempered — not negated — by the persistent memecoin/'casino' concern and unresolved legal fights (IRS broker rule, DAO liability, debanking). Optimism is about building real networks, explicitly NOT about speculative trading.

notes: This is an aggregated 'house' view across distinct authors with different beats — a regulatory/legal voice (Howey, lockups, RANP, DUNA, decentralization-as-limiting-principle), a ZK/cryptography voice (Jolt/Lasso, SNARK security), a DePIN/infra voice (energy, oracles, proving networks), a consumer/networks voice (Read Write Own, ownership, stablecoin payments), an IP/NFT voice (Story, The Everything Token), and academic research transcripts (MEV, stablecoin runs, gas pricing, governance/liquid democracy). Consensus is strongest on: decentralization-as-core-principle, stablecoins, crypto×AI, ZK, and anti-memecoin sentiment. KEY REVERSAL weighted by recency: regulatory posture shifted from acute alarm (2023–2024) to cautious optimism after the Nov 2024 US election. Prediction markets warrant a note: dismissed as a dead use case in a Sept-2023 research transcript ('didn't find a good use case') but viewed positively by 2024 as information-surfacing tools (Polymarket) — the recent view governs. 'Active addresses' is explicitly distrusted as a user metric; the team's own estimate is 30–60M real monthly users. Conviction scores are intensity of stated excitement/concern, not probabilities. Many research-transcript items are individual-speaker views surfaced by a16z rather than firm-wide positions; I marked the weaker-attribution ones (MEV centralization, stablecoin runs, SOL) accordingly.
